title: cylindropuntia ganderi subsp. catavinensis
intro: Cylindropuntia ganderi subsp. catavinensis, also known as Catavi Cholla, is a species of cactus that belongs to the Opuntia genus. It is native to the highlands of Bolivia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and rheumatism, due to its antiseptic and analgesic properties. Herbal preparations of Catavi Cholla include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain, and promote wound healing.
